goal_definitions.text: Implement the add-text-retrieval-baselines OpenSpec change under the established project boundaries.

evidence_requirements.description: Focused and full local validation pass for text corpus loading, BM25, dense-text, hybrid/RRF, metrics, and diagnostic report behavior.
Generated reports and documentation remain diagnostic-only and keep final test, visual inference, hard-negative mining, and training out of scope.

constraints: Use OpenSpec artifacts as the source of truth for the active implementation phase.
Keep edits inside bounded repo-relative phase allowed paths.
Do not mutate .opsx-auto, .opsx-auto-config, .git, or paths outside the phase scope.
success_thresholds: All mandatory evidence requirements are fresh for the active phase.
The local validation wrapper passes without network, GPU, model download, staging, commit, merge, push, archive, or deployment.
completion_criteria: The active OpenSpec task list is complete.
pytest, Ruff, mypy, OpenSpec strict validation, and whitespace checks pass through the pinned validation wrapper.
A concise Chinese Human Brief records the phase outcome and remaining limitations.
privacy_boundaries: Do not expose secrets, credentials, tokens, SSH material, private IPs, or unsanitized private logs.
release_boundaries: No automatic staging, commit, merge, push, pull request, release, deployment, or external account action.
non_goal_statements: No ColPali or ColQwen inference in the opsx-auto prerequisite phase.
No Phase 2 retrieval implementation in the opsx-auto prerequisite phase.
No hard-negative mining, LoRA or QLoRA training, final test evaluation, or benchmark improvement claim.
No .opsx-auto store initialization during prerequisite preparation.
